name: synthetic-devops-fixture
description: Committed CI regression corpus. Entirely synthetic (checkout-api theme); includes near-miss distractors sharing vocabulary with the golden answers so questions are not gimmes. Labels are hand-written, deterministic ground truth.

memories.title: Incident: elasticache-prod connection exhaustion took down checkout
memories.content: 2025-11-03 outage: checkout-api exhausted Redis connections on elasticache-prod during the flash sale. Root cause: no connection pooling, each request opened a fresh client. Resolved by introducing a shared connection pool (max 50) in the redis client wrapper.

memories.title: Connection pool configuration for redis clients
memories.content: After the elasticache-prod incident we standardized redis connection pooling: max 50 connections, 5s acquire timeout, health-check on checkout. Introduced in commit a81f2c. This configuration was later reused by customer-api when they hit the same symptom.

memories.title: customer-api adopted the checkout redis pool settings
memories.content: customer-api saw rising redis latency in staging and adopted the pool configuration from commit a81f2c wholesale (max 50, 5s acquire timeout). Same fix, second service. Pattern: connection exhaustion symptoms should always be checked against pool limits first.

memories.title: checkout-api session storage lives in Redis, app data in PostgreSQL
memories.content: checkout-api stores user sessions in Redis (elasticache-prod) and application data in PostgreSQL (rds-checkout-prod). These are complementary, not redundant: sessions need TTL semantics, orders need transactions.

memories.title: checkout-api listens on port 8080 behind the ALB
memories.content: checkout-api serves HTTP on port 8080; the ALB terminates TLS and forwards to the target group on that port. Health check path is /healthz on the same port.

memories.title: Deploys moved from Jenkins pipelines to GitHub Actions
memories.content: The old Jenkins deploy pipeline for checkout-api was retired in favor of GitHub Actions with OIDC into AWS. The Jenkinsfile is gone; deploys are now .github/workflows/deploy.yml triggered on tag push. Rollback is re-running the workflow with the previous tag.

memories.title: checkout-api requires Python 3.12
memories.content: checkout-api pins Python 3.12 in pyproject.toml; 3.11 fails on the tomllib usage in config loading. CI matrix runs 3.12 only.

memories.title: Evaluated Redis as a response cache and rejected it
memories.content: We considered caching rendered cart responses in Redis but rejected it: invalidation complexity outweighed the latency win at current traffic. Revisit if p99 exceeds 400ms. No connection to the incident work - this was a separate evaluation.

memories.title: PostgreSQL connection pool sizing via pgbouncer
memories.content: rds-checkout-prod sits behind pgbouncer in transaction mode, default pool 20. This is unrelated to the redis pool work; postgres never had an exhaustion incident.

memories.title: ALB idle timeout raised to 120s for report downloads
memories.content: The ALB idle timeout for checkout-api was raised from 60s to 120s because large report downloads were being cut off. Unrelated to port or health-check configuration.

memories.title: Always verify an image tag exists before committing a manifest
memories.content: Hard rule: before any commit that sets a container image tag or Helm chart tag, verify the artifact exists in the registry (docker manifest inspect). Learned from a prod outage caused by a typoed tag.

memories.title: PyPI READMEs must use absolute URLs
memories.content: PyPI renders the README standalone, so relative links (LICENSE, docs/foo.md) 404. Always use absolute GitHub URLs in anything published to PyPI.

memories.title: CI that publishes the repo root must exclude tooling dirs
memories.content: Before committing dev tooling (.claude/, hooks, logs) to a repo, check how it publishes. If CI mirrors the repo root to a public target (S3, gh-pages), tooling leaks publicly unless excluded.

memories.title: Terraform S3 backend bootstrap is a chicken-and-egg
memories.content: Terraform cannot manage its own remote state bucket; create the bucket with the CLI before terraform init. Standard bootstrap sequence documented in the platform runbook.

questions.query: which incident caused the redis connection pool configuration
questions.notes: Cause chain: incident -> pool config. Both memories needed to answer why the config exists.

questions.query: where else did we reuse the connection pool fix from checkout
questions.notes: Reuse question: must surface the adopting service and the origin config.

questions.query: where does checkout-api store user sessions
questions.notes: Distractors distractor-redis-cache and distractor-postgres-pool share redis/postgres vocabulary.

questions.query: what port does checkout-api listen on
what did we use for deploys before github actions
questions.notes: Temporal what-was-true-before question answerable from one memory.

questions.query: which python version does checkout-api require and why
what should I check before committing claude tooling to a repo that deploys to s3
questions.notes: Cross-cutting wisdom retrieval; distractor-wisdom-terraform shares s3/aws vocabulary.

questions.query: rules to verify before a deploy commit with an image tag
questions.notes: Multi-namespace search: deploy-migration and distractors share deploy vocabulary.
